items.title: Significant organized crime investigation nets major seizure of drugs, cash and more for CFSEU-BC : My Cariboo Now
items.content_html: Police say a commercial truck driver was arrested at the Sunshine Hills Shopping Plaza in Delta on May 3, 2026, during what investigators believe was a drug-related exchange involving another individual from Vancouver.

items.title: [BC Gov News] Inviting residents of 100 Mile House to comment on timber supply review
items.content_html: **_Summary_** * _People in 100 Mile House and surrounding communities are invited to read the timber supply review public discussion paper for the 100 Mile House Timber Supply Area (TSA) and provide feedback_ * _Feedback will help the chief forester determine a new allowable annual cut (AAC), which_ _defines the volume of timber that may be sustainably harvested from the TSA each year_ \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ The public is invited to give feedback on the upcoming timber supply review for the 100 Mile House Timber Supply Area (TSA). ## Structure fire north of Williams Lake near Deep Creek Published 1:50 pm Monday, June 22, 2026 **By Staff Williams Lake Tribune** (Pearly May/Facebook photo) (Pearly May/Facebook photo) The Wildwood Volunteer Fire Department was on the scene of a structure fire north of Williams Lake late Monday morning June 22. Providing mutual assistance was the Williams Lake Fire Department with a tender, engine and a dozen members attending the scene near Xat’sull First Nation’s Deep Creek community by the Esso gas station on Kragbak Road.
